about v16

Back to the Core, Forward in Form

🔹 Illustrij 16

is a fusion ~ a conscious pause between old and new. She remains true to herself and recognizes her original reflection more than ever. What seems like a return is also progress: a rediscovery of the origin with a new perspective.

The semi-realistic base with a subtle anime touch remains, but now with a touch more video game aesthetic ~ stylized but tangible, inspired by character designs that balance between playable figure and digital fantasy. The look is more oriented towards the origins ~ before the big 2.5D shift ~ and picks up the iconic face shape of previous versions: familiar, rounder, clearer.

This time, the special focus is on the realistic reaction of the body: skin that responds to movement, light and shadow that create depth and presence. SFW and NSFW are still supported - but with more emphasis on the body through credible lighting and play of form. v16 wraps itself in a cocoon of “Back to the Roots”, interwoven with a sweet-cheeky duality ~ sweet but cheeky, like at the beginning. From this inner reconciliation a new self emerges: a balance between nostalgia and artistic development.

about v14

🔹 Illustrij v14 ~ she moves with us
While preparing the showcase, she caught me all over again ~ and that’s what I love most about Illustrij.
We meet her with or without expectations ~ it doesn’t matter. She surprises, reveals new shades of herself, and stirs that quiet curiosity that pulls us deeper.

She dances with the prompt ~ playful, bold, sensual. Not loud, but vividly alive.
Her look is familiar, yet this time she feels more tangible than ever ~ softer fills, intentional lighting, and a glow that embraces instead of overwhelming.
Her clothing? Almost like skin ~ clinging to her with every movement, emotion, breath. As if it wants to sway with her, feel with her, live with her.

Illustrij v14 doesn’t live in the frame.
She lives the frame.

🔸 Showcase Note:
All showcase images are rendered at 1040 × 1510 ~ because she just wanted to show a little more of herself.
A bit more room, a bit more motion. That’s so Illustrij: more pixels, more presence, more of that signature sway. 🩰✨

If she twirled her way into ur heart too ~ she’d love a ♥️.
And so would I. ✨🩰

I am happy if u enjoy and stay curious abt ur results ~♥

about v13

🖤 Tiny update ~ big heart ✨

Hey friends~

although I wasn't planning on doing a revision so quickly, a quiet spark inside me made me want to update Illustrij with a lil more soul ~ especially in her hands, her gestures, her presence. She's still herself, just a touch more expressive.

✨ At the same time, on-site generation has brought new challenges ~ and one of them is staying true to her unique face without needing facefixes. That’s why I’ve been merging and refining more often lately. It's not just tinkering ~ it’s me listening closely to her, shaping her gently, and trying to bring her essence through without compromise.

It’s a small change, but it carries a lot of love. Maybe it’s just a subtle shift… or maybe it’s the detail someone was waiting for. Either way, I couldn’t keep it to myself ♡

But I didn't want to just overwrite her former self. So: The current version is now available for free download as a small thank u and souvenir ~ and in a few days the updated version will gently take her place in the on-site generation, assuming we make it through the auction.

Thank u so much for being here through all her forms ♡ Every detail, every evolution ~ it means the world to share it with u.

Let’s keep creating ~ together, gently, beautifully.

With 💖 ~ Reij

All showcase images are in on site resolution 832 x 1216 with hires x1,526 ~ without Adetailer

✨ Hey friends~

Back again with a new version of my precious comic-anime hybrid: Illustrij v12 🌸

U know how much I love that digital art rendering that leans into soft 3D, with a touch of lightplay and emotion. And with v12, she’s blooming more than ever ~ with more depth, warmth and expression.
She’s not just a model ~ she’s a little soul. A vibe. A mood. A presence.
I don’t just build tools ~ I shape characters. And she, like the others, feels like her.

🖤 This version has a stronger sense for light, character design and stylistic harmony ~ especially suited for natural and studio-style shots. I focused the previews more on my current fav: soft retro-grunge aesthetics 🧃🌾 but she’s still versatile like always! (The other styles are all still possible, give it a try~)

If she speaks to u ~ a like, a comment or a creation means the world.

about v5

hey guys, ☺

the gap between v4 and v5 was only a few days ago, but I've been "tinkering around" a bit and didn't want to keep it from you.

Trying to work on getting the quality closer to hires without hires - but there are still a few steps to go ^^
For people who have good hardware and can generally use a lot of Loras etc. at the same time the difference might not be that big, but I feel for the people who have small graphics cards :I 
The quality isn't perfect, but I've been working on it a bit ~ soft filling, clean renderings ~ the plan was to create an own style, subtly painted ~ anime-based, but still kissed by reality.

I hope you like the merge and I'm really looking forward to your results ♥

Belonging to the style u prefer u can switch the promptings 4 sure by ur own taste. In general, I mostly use "masterpiece,best quality,amazing quality,very aesthetic,absurdres,newest,detailed eyes" for general quality prompting, and besides depending on whether I want more anime or anime semi 3D touched I am writing "A professional,high-quality,hyper-realistic portrait of a woman, 3D" for the semianime 3D version and "A professional,high-quality portrait of a woman" for anime e.g. or as a conclusion for semi-anime:

"masterpiece, best quality, high resolution, very aesthetic, absurdres, newest, professional, high-quality, hyper-realistic, portrait of a woman, looking at viewer, detailed eyes, realistic body,"

and neg.:

"(lowres:1.2), (worst quality:1.4), (low quality:1.4), (bad anatomy:1.4), multiple views, jpeg artifacts, artist name, censored, young, 2D"

Sometimes I either leave out the negatives completely or write, for example, "lowres, worst "aesthetic, bad quality, worst quality, bad anatomy, sketch, jpeg artifacts, ugly, young, soft rendering, face marks"

depending on whether I use Euler or Euler a as sampler ^^

Usually I structure my prompts based on this:

  • subject description: who or what can be seen? (what does she/it look like? hair, eyes, clothing, description of the scene),

  • light and shadow description,

  • which angle, perspective, motion? quality

and

  • detailed description

or the quality at the very beginning and then the rest of the structure :)

If u're tasty for more? Here is a lil insight into the prompt structure: https://civitai.com/articles/10509/il-portreij-step-by-step

1062439.name: Illus Scores
1062439.description:
Update! (Well, not really, i just think adding a new embedding here is a better idea 😅)

n0t3xt: a quick attempt at a negative embedding to remove any text and signature. Does not work perfectly, but still, it helps.

raf1n33:

painterly, watercolor (medium), limited palette, vivid colors, no lineart, chromatic aberrations

For an upcoming test, i wanted to group together some words to facilitate weighting them as one.

UnNamedN3g:

This is a fully experimental negative embedding for UnNamedIXL. It was built by manually piecing together the first 16 tokens from 3 ACTI negative embeddings (neg_realism512, FastNegative V2, bad prompt), together with tokens for "boring, 2d, cg". This gives nice results but will tend to push the character to the center plus facing viewer.

PS: it does not yield good results on-site :-(

S0ft:

lineart, thick lineart, painterly, painting /(medium/), realistic, cartoon, western comics

So, those keywords may not do much on some models, especially anime heavy ones, but since i worked on a V2 of AnBan Shin based on a more western look, i made this embedding for my own usage (thanks @Ssssslippery for the trick!)

cr1sp:

vibrant, stunningly beautiful, crisp, detailed, sleek, ultramodern, subtle shadows, high contrast, cinematic, ultra detailed, intricate, professional

I have been using the same set of key words to help generating better stuff using HoJ, i have been tired typing or copy/pasting them, so, here is an embedding 😅

F4st:

dynamic pose, interesting angle, eye catching composition, depth of field, forced perspective

Wants a dynamic pose for your character and don't want to bother remembering a good combination of prompt to get it?

Just try F4st!

Lower the CFG to 4 for more creativity, put just this embedding in the prompt, the character and the background and run a few generation with random seed 👌

Can be great to get a first good step for ControlNet or as-is 😉

Previously:

So, i like the idea behind PonyScores, which help me not bother with remembering all the needed tags to be added for a good picture... and since i started playing with Illustrious, i created these helper embedding for a quick prompt :D

IllusP0s:

masterpiece, best quality, amazing quality, very aesthetic, absurdres, newest

IllusN3g:

bad quality, worst quality, worst detail, sketch, censored, watermark, signature, artist name

If you want to give it a try, just add both of those at the beginning of your positive and negative prompt and don't bother more than that, it should do the trick :D

PS: in ComfyUI, once the file is in the embedding folder, you may need to restart ComfyUI to have it detect the embedding in the CLIP Text Encode nodes:

1065154.name: Smooth Embeddings
1065154.description:
SMOOTH EMBEDDINGS

This is a set of quality enhancing embeddings for NoobAI, Illustrious/SDXL and Pony models.

No need for more quality prompts. They should be enough for getting great results!

I recommend using both positive and negative embeddings together.

NoobAI Checkpoints

  • Smooth Positive NoobAI / Smooth Negative NoobAI

Recommended only for NoobAI trained models since these embeddings use some tokens that are only recognized by NoobAI Checkpoints.

Can be very aggressive so don't use it together with a gigantic wall of prompts, specially when using the negative embedding.

So use:

  1. SmoothNoob_Quality on your Positive Prompt for NoobAI Models

  2. SmoothNoob_Negative on your Negative Prompt for NoobAI Models

Illustrious Checkpoints

  • Smooth Pos. Illustrious (+) / Smooth Neg. Illustrious

Recommended for Illustrious trained models. It gives great quality while adding gorgeous lighting effects.

Smooth Pos. Illustrious+ is much less aggressive on lighting effects now so it should give you more control over prompts that influence light.

So use:

  1. Smooth Quality on your Positive Prompt for Illustrios/SDXL Models

  2. Smooth Negative on your Negative Prompt for Illustrios/SDXL Models

Pony Checkpoints

  • Smooth Pos. Pony / Smooth Neg. Pony

Recommended for Pony trained models.

These embeddings use most tokens related to quality for Pony Checkpoints, so use only on those.

So use:

  1. SmoothQualityPony on your Positive Prompt for Pony Models

  2. SmoothNegativePony on your Negative Prompt for Pony Models

There are a lot of high quality Embeddings on the site already, feel free to use as many others as you want together with these.

EXTRA: SMOOTH NEGATIVE HANDS

We all agree that Hands are ANNOYING to make on AI art.

After getting a few messages asking what prompts/embeddings to use to fix hands I decided to make a Negative Embedding for it.

Its very light and should not affect the quality or style of your image in anyway.

It uses tokens of NoobAI, Illustrious and Pony models so use it with any Checkpoint you want.

Its not a 100% fool proof. Results will vary depending on what combinations of Loras and Checkpoints you are using.

So use:

  1. SmoothNegative_Hands on your Negative Prompt on any NoobAI, Illustrios or Pony Models

Have Fun!

modelVersions.name: Coconut
modelVersions.description:
• Coconut Milk

  • recommended to try Euler A and DDPM samplers first. Try others too, you may notice different styling. (Euler A, 3-5 cfg, ~28 steps)

  • try characters and artist tags, surprisingly well utilized

  • this version really likes detailed prompts and seems to have decent prompt responsiveness. Furthermore, the styling range seems fairly versatile, with adequate prompt direction. The default styling is somewhat 'anime semi-cgi' oriented.

  • utilize style tags to your advantage, positive + negative prompts (this applies to all versions). ie 'anime' in positive, and 'realistic' in negative or vice versa.

  • if you have problems with eyes with this version, try hi-res fix and/or adjusting your prompt to orient the character(s) in a closer manner. Check for things like 'full body' to remove or reduce weights on or any conflicting prompting. Alternatively, you can add or remove certain character/artist tags as support.

  2. V7 - https://www.mage.space/play/0a2a61c6d4a668ef51f552df0231067d

https://sinkin.ai/m/RR7Vrj4

Want to send some support? (Buy a cup at Ko-fi)

FYI VAE IS BAKED IN

Version 3 has https://huggingface.co/madebyollin/sdxl-vae-fp16-fix baked in.

RealCartoon-XL is an attempt to get some nice images from the newer SDXL. It still is a bit soft on some of the images, but I enjoy mixing and trying to get the checkpoint to do well on anything asked of it. This model level is definitely pushing my computer, so takes a bit longer to actually get it things tested and mixed :)

The mission is the same of the SD1.5 versions. Which is to get a checkpoint to pretty much do well on prompts while looking good with good variety

I hope you all enjoy it! Please review and share your images. I very much appreciate the support with the downloads and feedback (THANK YOU ALL). Never thought it would get this much attention.

The Process:


The starting checkpoints for merging were a couple of top ones of course (The checkpoints do/did not have restrictions on checkpoint mergers. Some of these are in my recommended resources as well). I also baked in the VAE (sdxl_vae.safetensors).

Currently this checkpoint is at its beginnings, so it may take a bit of time before it starts to really shine. I will continue to update it as time progresses...but I do hope you all enjoy it as it develops.

The Settings:

Still figuring out SDXL, but here is what I have been using:

  • Width: 1216 (normally would not adjust unless I flipped the height and width)

  • Height: 832

  • Sampling Method: "Eular A" and "DPM++ 2M Karras" are favorites.

  • Sampling steps: 30 - 55 normally (30 being my starting point, but going up to 55 and even 60 a lot of the time)

  • Hires.fix settings: Upscaler (R-ESRGAN 4x+, 4k-UltraSharp most of the time), Hires Steps (10), Denoising Str (0.34 - 0.45 normally), Upscale (1.5 or 2 does well)

  • Clip Skip: 2

Some settings I run on the web-Ui to help get the images without crashing:

set COMMANDLINE_ARGS=--medvram --no-half-vae --opt-sdp-attention

modelVersions.name: V7
modelVersions.description: Well, this checkpoint was well overdue for an update. Overall I was liking the results and I hope you all enjoy! I love all the images that were shared with the previous version and hope this version does well for you all as well.

modelVersions.name: V6
modelVersions.description: This one was interesting to work on. My computer did not want to work with me lol; but finally started working. So, for this version I am just trying to improve the overall look and response. SDXL is rather cool in that it does better with it range based on the prompt...but I still like the creative look SD 1.5 does initially (well at least with my other RealCartoon checkpoints that is, lol). ANYWAY, this version responds well and does well with details....and if you prompt for more of a realistic look it does well. Anyway, I hope you all enjoy!! Last update for the year :P ....my be busy with new child at this point...so may not get to update for a minute.

modelVersions.name: V5
modelVersions.description: It has been awhile. After some time and some RAM updates; finally was able to get SDXL running smoother. Thus, was able to work on the XL version of RealCartoon. I think it is starting to get the overall look I am wanting. It responds rather well; and comes out a bit clearer. The interesting part about a SDXL checkpoints is that they have a bit more range...so it takes a bit of getting used too. Overall though, I liked the outputs from the common prompts I use on SD1.5. I hope you all enjoy!!

modelVersions.name: V4
modelVersions.description: I think this update is starting to get the XL version of RealCartoon to work similar to the 1.5 version but of course with "more". Hopefully I will be able to start branching it to get some more "Pixar" and "Anime" looks. I hope you all enjoy it! Again thank you all for the image sharing and reviews! Cannot believe the RealCartoon Models have broken 100k downloads.

modelVersions.name: V3
modelVersions.description:

I decided to use this VAE because it seems to do better and run faster. Overall I have enjoyed the results. I hope you do as well.

1353314.name: Animij
1353314.description:
v10 showcase resolution 832×1536 / no Hires, no ADetailer ☺ a little more about v10 below 🔽

💌

A small thank you —~

to those who tinker with me,
the dreamers,
the enjoyers :)

I hope you're having fun exploring v10. 💗

Animij

Hey guys,

u asked abt it. 😊 Now I present to u: Animij ~ retro-modern soft cored. 4 different styles ~ moaarrrrrr possibilities?! 😋

For a long time I asked myself why merge an anime model ~ when there are a wealth of great models that serve this genre?

Ur voice is important to me, so I took the plunge. U know me, I like things different, and so Animij spawns as an anime style that may not suit everyone's taste ~ but maybe urs?

Animij is coming

  • soft

  • drawn

  • vibrant

  • retro touched

  • with a touch of eroticism

  • minimalistic, but detailed

  • and full of emotions

Tasty for more? Feel free to play around ~ every version comes with her own playground ☺

💌 NEW

✦ Version 10 —~ Core ID

💙 Animij is an ID model.

What does that mean?

💙Animij has character ;)

• clear subject separation
• soft isofocus / gentle background falloff

• light defines form
• soft-gloss skin highlights
• clean, controlled speculars

• color interaction (warm ↔ cool)
• background supports subjects

• playful / confident posing
• slight perspective presence

• restrained, accessible expressions
• calm / soft / slightly playful emotion

• clean edges + soft rendering balance
• semi-anime aesthetic

• lighting with behavior
• flexible character identity

💙 v10 was created from a feeling of gratitude.

So many heartwarming words touched me.

…they stayed.

This is how v10 emerged ☺

For this moment ~ I’m a little quieter in words

So I let the images speak.

I hope you enjoy.

—~ Reij ♥

Reij's Animij v10 Sweetspots

This is how I personally like to use Animij v10.

UniPC Karras

  • 24–28 steps

  • CFG 5.0–5.5

  • 832 x 1216 / 832 x 1536

clean / balanced / soft finish

DPM++ 2S a Karras

  • 24–28 steps

  • CFG 5.0–5.3

  • 832 x 1216 / 832 x 1536

more punch / stronger contrast / more energy

DPM++ 2M Karras

  • 26–30 steps

  • CFG 5.1–5.6

  • 832 x 1216 / 832 x 1536

stable / clean / slightly sharper

Euler a

  • 20–24 steps

  • CFG 5.5–6.5

  • 832 x 1216 / 832 x 1536

more playful / more variation

✦ Version 9

"Sparks Found in the Space Inbetween Shadows"

hey friends, ☺

i’m not a fan of comparisons, but what stands out with v9 is that it works both with various quality tags and negative prompts ~ and also without them.

Adetailer? optional.
in some showcase images i simply didn’t use it ^^

hires, i remain a fan of upscaling, just to give the model more room to unfold. but the model can be used without it.

steps? i prefer around 40, but 20 have their own charm too. entirely up to your taste ^^

for different hardware, whether onsite or local. i’ll only be able to test onsite later, so it was important to me to try the model locally first with smaller settings :)

sampler? feel free to experiment ~ currently i’m using Euler a and DPM++ 2M Karras.

✅ ladies? yes
✅ gentlemen? yes

✅ 🥵 nsfw? yes
✅ 🦊 furry? yes

i love it ♥
i hope you enjoy the new playground just as much :)

with ♥ Reij

✦ Version 7

| ♥ | Specs: Euler a / DPM++ 2M Karras · 30+ steps · 832 x 1216 · CFG 4-7 · Hires x 1,5

| ♥ | Quality Tags (option examples):
  | + | masterpiece, high_quality, highres
  | ~ | worst quality, bad quality,

✦ Version 1.2

hey friends,
your feedback is important to me ☺

That's why there is now the first Animij update ^-^
v1.2 retains the style of v1, is a little more refined in the rendering and spawns with more options to use quality tags or leave them out to suit your taste ☺

I hope you have a lot of fun being creative
with ♥ Reij

20+ steps

832 x 1216

quality tags & negative prompt optional ~ use or omit as preferred

prompt examples:

✦ Version 6

Like the previous versions of Animij, v6 is a separate model, not an update ☺

~ blending anime, semi-realistic, and comic-inspired elements.

⚠️

♥ settings:

🛠️ General settings I like to use: Euler a, 30+ steps, and a resolution around 832 × 1216. But of course ~ u’re warmly invited to be creative here too! 🌸✨

During merging, special care was taken so you don’t need any quality tags for great results ~ and u can even leave out the negative prompt if u like.

Just create the way u love 💖

Most importantly: I want to give both prompt pros and new creative AI minds the same freedom to explore – just prompt whatever comes to mind and let the magic happen 💖

I am happy if u enjoy and stay curious abt ur results ♥ best regards Reij

✦ Version 5

hey friends ☺

V5 spawns soft kissed by V1 ~ not back to the roots but with retro shine and velvety soft ~ comes with more freedom in the quality prompt design ~ more user-friendly for beginners and prompt professionals, whether realism or toon, V5 stays true to her style.

I am happy if u enjoy and stay curious abt ur results

with ~♥ Reij

Regarding the quality tags, u are cordially invited to let off steam creatively or to give more freedom ~ according to ur taste ☺

✦ Version 4

from minimalist to fine details ~ ur prompt, ur fantasy ~ remains anime-based even with realistic attention. Through self-determined prompting more play in style variations: from matt to bright, accentuated or rich in detail, there is always a touch of detail, whether as a classic 2D or with more depth.

I am happy if u enjoy and stay curious abt ur creations

✦ Version 3 ~ drawn symbiosis

v3 is the most drawn version so far. The focus is on the connection between AI and creative intuition ~ a quiet, painterly collaboration.

Born digital, but with the spirit of analogue: Every stroke appears as if it were drawn by hand ~ clear, accentuated, deliberately placed. It's not paper, but a canvas. Not a brush, but still a brush stroke.

v3 brings the “Drawn Touch” back to the fore ~ as a homage to drawing itself and as an expression of a new aesthetic: where AI does not replace, but accompanies.

✨ The Art of Animij ~ where details speak ✨

In Animij, details whisper of emotion ~ a stroke of storytelling. Eyes shimmer with depth, reflecting unspoken dreams. Light and shadow dance across soft gradients, giving each face a warmth that feels almost alive.

The magic lies in the small things: the delicate blush of a cheek, the glint of light on a ring, the way colors breathe with life. Nothing is static~ every detail wanna invite u: wanna come deeper? making each piece feel like a moment suspended between fantasy and reality.

✨ Wanna look closer? The story is in the details.

1400090.name: People's Works +
1400090.description:
快速上手 | Quick Start

这是什么？ | What is this?

  • People's Works是一个实验性的微调模型系列，最初基于一个由Pony V6 XL生成的图片构成的数据集。这个模型的数据集由数千张AI社区用户发布的图片和作者使用AI合成的图片构成，经过人工筛选、编辑、修改和标注后用于训练。除此之外还有一个超过2000张由照片、游戏CG和3d渲染图像构成的辅助数据集，用于额外补充知识。

  • People’s Works is an experimental fine-tuned model series, originally based on a dataset composed of images generated by Pony V6 XL. The dataset consists of several thousand images published by AI community users, along with images synthesized by the author using AI. These images were manually curated, edited, modified, and annotated before being used for training. In addition, there is an auxiliary dataset of over 2,000 images composed of photographs, game CGs, and 3D renders, used to provide supplementary knowledge.

模型功能 | Model features

  • 本系列模型主要功能是帮助基础模型在不使用画师串、较少的质量提示词的条件下获得相对稳定的风格化图像，为提示词节约token空间。

  • 使用人工挑选和标注的数据集强化训练正面和负面审美提示词。

  • 模型针对flat color、realistic等特定风格肌理进行了强化。

  • 对人物的年龄、族裔、肤质等特征的精细化控制。

  • 使用了较高的训练分辨率，使基础模型在高清修复时表现更好。

  • 通过手工修复图片的方式降低了模型的某些细节瑕疵出现的概率。

  • The primary function of this series of model is to help the basemodel generate relatively stable, stylized images, without artist keywords or long quality tags, freeing up token space for prompts.

  • By using manually selected and annotated datasets, the model strengthens both positive and negative aesthetic tags training.

  • The model includes targeted enhancements for specific visual textures and styles, such as flat color and realistic.

  • It enables finer control over character attributes, including age, ethnicity, and skin texture.

  • A higher training resolution is used, improving the basemodel’s performance during high-res upscaling.

  • By manually editing the images, the likelihood of certain flaws appearing in the model’s outputs has been reduced.

使用方法 | Usage

positive:

masterpiece, best quality, very aesthetic

negative:

low quality, displeasing

更新记录 | Change log

v9

  1. 更改了系列名称。自这个版本起，训练集中来自Pony v6 XL的图片已经在所有AIGC内容中占比不足1/3。随着越来越多的新模型出现，我有计划在明年将这个系列拓展到其他模型上。为了避免未来用户使用上的混乱和误解，这个系列从这个版本起更改命名。
    The series name has been changed. Starting from this version, images sourced from Pony v6 XL make up less than one third of the training data across all AIGC content. As increasing number of new models are emerging, I plan to expand this series to other models next year. To avoid potential confusion and misunderstanding for users in the future, the series name has been changed starting from this version.

  2. 这个版本的训练方式是直接训练LoCon，而非训练Checkpoint后再抽取LoRA。模型相较前一个版本效果更强。
    This version is trained directly as a LoCon, rather than training a checkpoint first and then extracting a LoRA. Compared to the previous versions, the model delivers stronger effects.

  3. v9全系列使用1536分辨率的训练集。现在使用这个Lora生成图片时支持单边768-1536的分辨率，使用高清修复时也可以尝试更高的denoise参数了。
    All v9 models use a 1536-resolution training set. When generating images with this LoRA, single-side resolutions from 768 to 1536 are now supported. When using high-res fix, you can also try higher denoise values.

  4. 对训练图片调色。现在模型在没有指定色彩时更倾向于生成暖色调的图片，并且色彩的饱和度略微提高。较暗的场景明暗对比更大了。
    Color adjustments were applied to images. When no specific color is specified, the model now tends to produce warmer tones, with slightly increased saturation. Darker scenes also have stronger contrast between light and shadow.

  5. 之前版本的数据集中，人物鼻子的画法不统一。出于作者本人的兴趣，手工修改了其中约300幅图片，并暂时排除了约200幅来不及修改的图片。现在人物的鼻子有鼻翼了。
    In earlier versions of dataset, nose depiction was inconsistent. Out of personal interest, the author manually modified around 300 images and temporarily excluded about 200 images that could not be edited in time. Characters’ noses now have nose wings.

  6. 删除了旧版本中数百张过时的低质量训练数据。
    Hundreds of outdated, low-quality images from older versions of dataset have been removed.

  7. 添加了一个新的实验性数据集： 使用真人相片作为引导，现在你可以使用以下年龄和族裔标签了：
    A new experimental dataset has been added. Using real photographs as guidance, you can now use the following age and ethnicity tags:

child, teenage, adult, mature
Caucasian, Asian, Indian, African

  • 我的标签设计优先选择Danbooru数据集中已经存在的标签，尽管其中很多只有很少量的数据，在原版模型中几乎无法触发。重新启用了已经被删除的danbooru词条Caucasian和teenage，增设了adult和African两个标签。此外，loli和shota因为其文化背景中强烈的性暗示倾向，这两个词条被完全替换，根据具体情况分流入child和teenage。
    My tag design prioritizes labels that already exist in the Danbooru dataset, even though many of them had very limited data and are therefore almost impossible to trigger in the base models. The previously removed Danbooru tags Caucasian and teenage have been re-enabled, and two new tags, adult and African, have been added. Additionally, due to the strong sexual connotations of loli and shota in their cultural context, these tags have been completely replaced and redistributed into child and teenage depending on the situation.

v8

肌理更新：强化了以下tag的学习：

Texture Update: The following tags have been reinforced in training:

realistic, photorealistic, flat color,
shiny skin, matte skin, shiny hair,

请注意，在danbooru数据集中有很多个用于描述“照片”和“接近照片的风格”的tag。我在训练集中统一标注这些图片为“photorealistic”。但是使用danbooru训练集训练的SDXL模型大多并不能很好地画出写实图像，因此“photorealistic”只建议在较小权重下用于改变画面的肌理。“realistic”可以在高权重下正常运作。

Please note that Danbooru dataset contains multiple tags to describe "photo" or "photo-like styles". I’ve tagged all such images as “photorealistic” in dataset.

However, most SDXL models trained on the Danbooru dataset do not render realistic images well. “photorealistic” is only recommended at low weight, where it can help adjust texture rather than create realism images. The “realistic” tag can work properly at higher weight.

v8版本简介：

  • Pony: People's Works （ppw）是一个实验性的微调模型系列，数据集有约85%是收集自CivitAI上用户发表的AI生成图片。早期ppw的数据集最初建立在由pony v6生成图片的基础上，因此本系列模型生成的图片也带有pony diffusion的特征。

  • 本系列模型使用标准Danbooru标签，主要擅长生成中、近景风格化人像。它们的主要功能是使基础模型可以在不使用画师串、较少的质量提示词的条件下获得相对稳定的图像质量，为提示词节约token空间。
    本模型并非风格模型，在不同的提示词和生成条件下可能会有微妙的画风差异。

  • Pony: People's Works (ppw) is a experimental fine-tuned model series, approximately 85% of the dataset comes from AI-generated images published by users on CivitAI. Since the earlier ppw dataset was built on images generated by Pony V6, the outputs of this series also carry some characteristics of Pony Diffusion.

  • This series uses standard Danbooru tags and is mainly optimized for generating stylized portraits at medium and close range. The primary effect of this model series is to allow the basemodel to achieve relatively stable image quality, without artist keywords or long quality tags, freeing up token space for prompts.
    These models are not style LoRAs. There may be subtle stylistic variations depending on different prompts and generating conditions.

v7

v7版本对数据集结构做了较大幅度的调整，并且使用了不同的训练参数和训练策略，因此可能v7会不如原来的版本稳定。

The v7 version has undergone significant structural adjustments to the dataset, and utilizes different training parameters and strategies. As a result, v7 may be less stable than the previous versions.

v-pred模型在civitAI在线生成器上的表现和吐司的在线生成表现完全不一样，相同参数完全无法复现。我也不知道是为什么......

The v-pred model's performance on the CivitAI online generator is completely different from online generation on TensorArt. The results are entirely unreproducible with a same parameters. I have no idea why...

TensorArt version CivitAI ver. with same parameter on CivitAI with higher weight

v7版本简介：

这是一个在前作的数据集基础上发展而来的图像质量LoCon，约90%-95%图片数据来自CivitAI上发布的图片。

它使模型可以在不使用画师串、使用较少的质量提示词的条件下获得相对稳定的图像质量，节约出更多的token空间，同时它还可以修复一部分模型固有的生成瑕疵。（但是不包括手部）

因为数据集选取的原因，生成的图片会带有Pony的质感。但因为它并不指向任何特定的画师、风格和绘画技法，所以在不同的提示词、模型条件下可能会有微妙的画风差异。

This is a generation quality LoCon developed based on the dataset from the previous work. About 90%-95% of the image data comes from CivitAI.

It allows models to achieve relatively stable image quality without artist tags or using long quality prompts, freeing up more token space. Additionally, it can fix some inherent generation flaws of the model. (except for hands)

Due to the dataset selection, the generated images exhibit a Pony-like style. However, since it does not reference any specific artist, style, or painting technique, there may be subtle stylistic variations depending on different prompts and checkpoint conditions.

A cinematic fine-tuned model for high-detail, character-rich realism

"Illustrious Realism by klaabu" is a high-fidelity model fine-tuned on top of the Illustrious framework, enhanced for realism, texture depth, and stronger control over nuanced facial and body features. It has been tuned to preserve the cinematic essence of Illustrious while elevating clarity and dynamic lighting.

Native Resolution Range:

Outputs cleanly from 832×1216 up to 1536×2048, ideal for both portraits and full-body shots.

Recommended Inference Settings:

Sampler: DPM++ SDE Karras
CFG: 4.0
Steps: 20
Clip Skip: 1

Prompting Style:

For best results, use Illustrious-style prompting — avoid stylised scoring systems like Pony. The model performs best with clean, natural phrasing and descriptive realism.

Suggested Positive Tags:

masterpiece, best quality, ultra-detailed, realistic skin, intricate details, highres,

Recommended Negatives:

low quality, worst quality, blurry, (deformed:1.3), extra fingers, cartoon, 3d, anime, bad anatomy

Character Handling:

The model handles a wide range of character likenesses including many known to Illustrious. It works smoothly with compatible LoRAs — including SDXL LoRAs, Illustrious variants, and Pony.

Illustrious Realism is crafted for users seeking high-end realism with expressive tone, dynamic lighting, and compatibility with advanced prompting techniques — all within a flexible resolution range.

1595884.name: Hyphoria
1595884.description:
Enter the Five Tag Challenge and have an opportunity to win up to 50,000 buzz from me! A fun challenge I created to see what creative things people can make with a limited amount of tags and a way to continue paying back the community for all the support.

I have created some articles comparing v0.01 to v0.02 so it is easier to see the improvements and changes I have made (one for PG and one for everything else):

  • https://civitai.red/articles/29602/pg-comparing-hyphoria-v001-vs-v002

  • https://civitai.red/articles/29604/xxx-comparing-hyphoria-v001-vs-v002

The long awaited new version of Hyphoria is here, after almost a year of waiting, I present v1 v0.02!

When I made the first version I was unsure of its quality or if it would even be worth using, but you all really have seemed to enjoy using it and doing things well beyond what I would have imagined with it. I'm glad everyone has loved it but that also made it hard to come up with something to improve, there were always things to improve but with merging and even training it isn't easy to make something that is a categorical improvement at least on my limited budget and available time.

So after experimenting with other models (my real version on Illustrious and my Qwen Image model) I learned a ton and over the last half year I experimented and tried to find something that I liked and felt like an improvement while not being a step down in most ways. I wanted it to feel like the same model but better and that is where v0.02 came to be.

It has much better contrast compared to v0.01, which was washed out especially when you did img2img or hires. The background details often were non-sense or didn't resolve to anything useful, v0.02 in a lot of ways improves on that but can still be nonsense but that is SDXL/Illu for you.

The other major area of improvement was around higher resolution stability being able to reliably generate at 1.5 megapixels with little to no anatomy issues (at least compared to v0.01) and that translates to better stability when doing hires passes on images. In my testing I rarely see anatomy issues like extra body parts even after multiple hires passes (but the denoise and resolution settings you choose will affect that obviously). It should be noted that ultra wide resolutions or high resolution wide aspect ratio generations can have issues with duplication or anatomy warping (e.g. subject laying horizontally) for both versions, portrait aspect ratios are more stable and support higher resolution without issues in v0.02.

v0.02 Recommended Settings

  • Steps: 20-35

  • CFG: 3 (2.5-6)

  • Sampler: Euler a OR DPM++ 2M (Should be stable on most)

  • Scheduler: Should be stable on most

  • Resolution: 1mp to 1.5mp

  • Prompting: Less is more, I often don't even use quality tags outside of worst quality in the negatives. Quality tags can lead to a glossier and less flexible result, but sometimes more attractive poses and lighting, experiment.

v0.01 Recommended Settings

  • Steps: 20-35

  • CFG: 3 (2.5-6)

  • Sampler: DPM++ 2M SDE (or DPM++ 2M on CIVITAI Gen)

  • Scheduler: Karras

  • Resolution: I recommend 1024x1024 - 1280x1280 for normal generation. Supports up to 1536x1536 without breaking, but can suffer some body extension. Any aspect ratio that equals the same number of pixels should work.

  • Prompting: This model listens to your prompts, like really listens, so avoid the fluff and keep your prompts focused.

    • Recommended Positive: masterpiece, best quality, absurdres

    • Recommended Negative: worst quality, low quality

    • You can often omit pos or neg quality tags depending on what you're trying to make, grungy images for instance will want to pull out some of those quality tags. Some of the other quality tags do work if you want those types of images.

License & Use

SDXL - CreativeML Open RAIL++-M
Illustrious - https://freedevproject.org/faipl-1.0-sd/
NoobAI-XL - https://civitai.com/models/license/1140829

Copied the above license list from Plant Milk's page.

Tried to improve overall details and creativity of the model while keeping its prompt adherence and knowledge, unfortunately I did not have the time to train in new characters or artists and merging models that had that knowledge was not as reliable as I had hoped.

1772230.description:
🎀 What is Illustrij Evo?

Evo isn’t just an Illustrij update.
It’s not a patch. Not a sequel.

Evo is a NEW class. ♪

A second main.
A merge that doesn’t just work ~ it feels.
Right where things turn cozy, dreamy, soft ~ and yet detailed, expressive, and free.

If u know me as a gamer, u know: I was never just one main.
100% into more than one class ~ that’s my gamer setting.
And that’s exactly how Evo came to life.

🎀 Illustrij Evo Lvl3 NEW

| 🎀 | Specs: DPM++ 2M Karras· 30 Steps · 832×1216 CFG · 4-7 · Hires ×1.526 · SFW & NSFW

| 🎀 | update Lvl2

| 🎀 | with or without quality tags usable ☺ ur taste ~ UR CHOICE

quality tags option examples:

| + | masterpiece, best quality,

| ~ | worst quality, bad quality,

or

| + | masterpiece, best quality, ultra-detailed, 8k resolution, high dynamic range, absurdres, stunningly beautiful, intricate details, sharp focus, detailed eyes, cinematic color grading, high-resolution texture, nails,

| ~ | (worst quality:2), (low quality:2), (normal quality:2), bad anatomy, bad proportions, poorly drawn face, poorly drawn hands, missing fingers, extra limbs, blurry, pixelated, distorted, lowres, jpeg artifacts, watermark, signature, text, (deformed:1.5), (bad hands:1.3), overexposed, underexposed, censored, mutated, extra fingers, cloned face, bad eyes

I am happy if u enjoy and stay curious abt ur results ~♥ Reij

🎀 Illustrij Evo Lvl2

| 🎀 | no update !
A reinterpretation ~ back to the roots of my Illustrij, yet in a fresh new look. Striking a balance between anime and semi-realism, deeper, more tangible ~ walking the line between fantasy and real accents.

Less is more. And more is never enough?
That’s why: no quality tags, no negative prompts ~ just freedom for creatives, whether seasoned prompt pros or complete newcomers. The expression comes with the tags. A model made to inspire, not to restrict.

Specs: Euler a / DPM++ 2M Karras· 40 Steps · 832×1216 · Hires ×1.526 · SFW & NSFW

| 🎀 |💡 Extra tip: For minimal settings (e.g. 15-20 steps · DPM++ 2M Karras · CFG 4) I’d especially recommend using quality tags, as they can help stabilize and enrich the output.

| 🎀 | 💡 For those curious about the showcased ladies’ look:
Use tags like (mature) woman, perky breasts, large breasts, small waist, soft thighs, skin pressed, (...pick a COLOR...) lashes, long lashes, very awa ~ and then let ur imagination expand on it.

| 🎀 | 🔎 Quality Tags – simple example
My showcase runs without quality tags and without negative prompts.
But here’s how things might look with some common ones, inspired by Lizardon’s style:

+ Prompt:
masterpiece, best quality, 1girl, face, portrait, looking at viewer, closed mouth

– Prompt:
worst quality, bad quality

https://civitai.com/posts/21048917

👉 The difference? Adding tags like masterpiece or best quality often sharpens the render and gives a slightly more polished look. Negative tags like worst/bad quality help push the model away from low-res or unwanted outputs.

| 🎀 | The fun part: try it urself, compare with the clean version (no tags, no negatives), and see which style u vibe with most.

⚠️On Site Settings Evo Lvl 2 ❗?

I recommend using Euler a / DPM++ 2M Karras with 40 steps and Hires Fix at denoise 0.2, as it gives a more clean, detailed results ~ but of course, that’s a matter of taste. ☺

I am happy if u enjoy and stay curious abt ur reults ~♥ Reij

🎀 Illustrij Evo Lvl1

🎀 What makes Evo different

Evo doesn’t aim to impress ~ it wants to move with u.
It’s a merge for those who read between the lines.
Those who don’t just type “smile”, but feel which kind is meant:

  • ❌ Cheeseface

  • ✅ A gentle play in the eyes, real warmth, a cozy moment

Evo responds to emotion, gaze depth, subtle lighting.
Not overdone. Not overprocessed.
But intentionally thoughtful.

🎀 Flexible like a Skilltree

No quality tags?
Evo holds up. Strong identity. Clear style. No blur, no break.
Even with minimal prompts ~ it still feels cozy, soft, organic.

With quality tags?

1833737.name: [BSS] - 🌗 Equinox (IL Semi-Realistic model)
1833737.description:
Equinox is a flexible semi-realistic XL model.
Built on top of Illustrious‑XL foundations, Equinox delivers natural rendering with expressive anatomy, clean lighting, and excellent prompt understanding — without being locked into a specific visual style.

v4.0 17/11/2025 ↷

I spent a huge amount of time training and merging a multitude of LoRAs and Sliders. The goal was to fix specific issues and refine the generation process.

Global Refinement: Literally everything is a bit better. Hands, eyes, composition — the overall quality.

The 2.5D Aesthetic: Out of the box, v4.0 leans towards a beautiful, rich 2.5D style.

Flexibility: Despite the 2.5D default, the model is steerable. With the right prompting, you can push it towards Anime or close-to-Realism.

🌗 Introducing: Equinox v4.0 Alt Alongside the main version, I’m releasing an Alternative version.

Different Vibe: It shares the same DNA as v4.0 but features a distinct aesthetic.

Lighting & Contrast: The Alt version focuses on better, more nuanced lighting with slightly reduced contrast for a softer, more atmospheric look.

🎁 Exclusive Early Access As a thank you for your support, all Boosty subscribers get instant access to BOTH versions. (Note: Civitai will also have Early Access, but the full bundle is exclusively available for my supporters here).

Let me know which version you prefer in the comments! Share your creations!

v3.0 17/11/2025 ↷

Equinox v3.0 is a true major update, not just a small polish.
This version focuses on higher stability, cleaner faces, and more accurate prompt
following, while keeping the signature Equinox softness.

✨ Core Improvements

  • Stronger facial stability (eyes, lips, expressions)

  • More accurate anatomy

  • Improved color balance and lighting cohesion

  • More predictable prompt response

  • Reduced artifacts and distortions

  • And many others

An alternative version will be available to my Boosty subscribers. All the same changes
as in v3.0, but a different Clip model is used.

v2.5 15/09/2025 ↷

Equinox v2.5 isn’t a “huge revolution” — it’s more like polishing what already worked in v2.
✨ I focused on making everything cleaner, more stable, and prettier — from anatomy and textures to contrast.

Why not 3.0?
Because I don’t want to throw around big version numbers for tiny updates. The jump from v1 → v2 was massive, and I want v3 to feel the same way — a true wow moment, not just “some small tweaks.”
That doesn’t mean v2.5 was easier to make — honestly, it took just as much work as a full new release. I tested a ton of variations (some of them were drastically different),
but in the end, I picked the most balanced one that keeps Equinox’s aesthetic intact.

💜 Equinox v2.5 is the same flexible semi-realism you love — now even cleaner, more stable, and better for experiments.

v2.0 20/05/2025 ↷

✨ Output Quality

  • Sharper & Cleaner — improved detail rendering and overall clarity

  • Refined Anatomy — better proportions and natural forms

  • Balanced Lighting — reduced harsh contrasts and artifacts

🎭 Prompting & Control

  • More Flexible Prompting — adapts better to diverse concepts and styles

  • Improved Prompt Following — responds more closely to your instructions

  • Dual-Mode Realism:

    • Add “realistic” → more realistic results

    • Omit “realistic” → softer semi-realistic / anime-inspired images

📐 Resolution & Performance

  • High-Resolution Boost — excellent results up to 1536px

🛠 Roadmap & Extras

  • Coming Soon — dedicated Inpaint version of Equinox

  • Early Access — Equinox v2.0 already available on Boosty for subscribers

❤️ Personal Note

I’m very proud of how Equinox is evolving, and I plan to keep developing it even further.

  • Sampler: Euler A (Karras) / DPM++ 2M SDE (Karras)

  • Steps: 25–40

344513.name: Been Real Lazy (Realistic)
344513.description:
Welcome!

Thanks for dropping by!  This is my first merge that I made while trying to find something that was a notch or two away from photorealistic.  I have a couple other versions that were a little closer to 2.5D style that I may releases down the road if there's interest.  Overall the merge is a softer, pastel-ish kind of vibe that can produce good results with or without clothing.  It's also surprisingly under-control, likely due to optimizations made in its parent (and grandparent) checkpoints.

 

All sample images were generated with no LoRA or TI in the positive prompt, and only using BadDream and UnrealisticDream in the negative.  They do have Adetailer and Hires.fix turned on.  Faces still generally look decent without Adetailer, but the eyes have a tendency to be more pastel tones.  My usual settings in the screenshot below.  I'll try to post some samples with some of the other ingredients I usually add to give you an idea of what is possible. The 840000-ema VAE is baked in to this merge.

 

My Usual Settings / Other Tested Options

Sampler - DPM++ 2M SDE Karras / Restart / Euler A

Steps - 30--50 (30 for fast gens to test ideas, 50 for things I may save/post)

Resolution - 512x896, 512x768

 

Hires.fix:

Upscaler - Ultramix_balanced, 4x_Ultrasharp

Steps - 15 or 20

Denoise - .3-.4

Upscale by - 1.5 - 2.0

 

Special thanks to Frizzante and kaylazy for the wonderful work they've done on their models, and the inspiration!

modelVersions.name: v16.0
modelVersions.description:
All sample images are 100% LoRA-free

Pony v16.0 is here - and this one is the result of listening, stepping back, and rebuilding with intent.

After extensive feedback from users, I deliberately took a step back from the previous direction, re-evaluated the foundations, and rebuilt from there. The result is a version that feels fresh, flexible, and experimental, while still staying true to the CyberRealistic identity.

If you’re looking for a rock-solid, predictable workhorse, I still recommend:

  • v14.x or v15.x - both remain excellent and stable choices

v16.0, however, is where the exciting stuff starts. It’s for users who enjoy testing, experimenting, and discovering new behaviors and capabilities.

modelVersions.name: v15.0
modelVersions.description:
CyberRealistic Pony V15.0 is a light refinement of CyberRealistic Pony Catalyst v5.0 - not a major overhaul. If you’ve already used Catalyst, expect the same visual style and behavior, with a handful of subtle improvements focused on stability, cleanliness, and prompt control.

Think of Pony V15.0 as Catalyst v5.0 with a final polish pass: familiar, reliable, and just a bit smoother. For users coming from CyberRealistic Pony v14.1, it will feel like a meaningful step forward - but for Catalyst users, it’s a gentle, incremental upgrade.

modelVersions.name: v14.1
modelVersions.description:
The Evolution Continues
V14.1 is here, and it's a direct upgrade from V14.0, shaped by your feedback. This version tightens up how well prompts are followed, smooths out the style, and brings better consistency overall. You’ll get results that feel more natural and in sync with what you asked for, no matter the vibe.

What's new:

  • Improved prompt adherence and tag consistency

  • General polish and optimization based on community feedback

modelVersions.name: v14.0
modelVersions.description:
CyberRealistic Pony V14.0 — Realism Boost, Pony Energy Intact
V14.0 pushes realism significantly while keeping the Pony look alive—cleaner anatomy, steadier faces/hands, richer skin and hair detail, and more natural lighting.

modelVersions.name: v13.0
modelVersions.description:
V13.0 takes things in a slightly different direction. It dials back some of the moodiness and intensity from earlier versions, aiming for a smoother, more versatile feel when you’re working with it.

It’s not a straight-up upgrade. Think of it more as a tweak that brings a different vibe. If you liked the bold, dramatic style of the older builds, you might still stick with those. But if you’re after something a little softer and more balanced, this version might hit the spot.

There’s also some focused training under the hood to fix specific issues from past releases. That makes it a bit more stable for certain workflows.

Overall, V13.0 isn’t here to replace the earlier versions. It’s just another creative option to suit whatever mood or style you’re going for.

modelVersions.name: v12.7
modelVersions.description: Improved lighting dynamics with better contrast between light and dark areas, enhanced color accuracy and vibrancy, plus additional training for overall image quality refinement.

modelVersions.name: v12.5
modelVersions.description:
V12.5 reins things in—quite literally. V12 was a bold leap with intense natural tones and punch, but honestly? It overshot what I personally wanted. While some users appreciated the vibrant direction, I felt it strayed too far.

So, V12.5 pulls it back to a more balanced place—figuratively nestled between V11’s restraint and V12’s flair. You still get the realism and improvements, but with more subtlety and control. Less turbo, more pony.

modelVersions.name: v12.0
modelVersions.description: CyberRealistic Pony V12 brings improved natural tones and training from Pony Catalyst.

modelVersions.name: v11.0
modelVersions.description:
CyberRealistic Pony V11 is here - the spiritual successor to the much-whispered-about Catalyst beta. After a barrage of messages like “Why is Catalyst better than the last full release?!” and “Just release it already!”, I gave in... sort of.

Catalyst wasn’t officially released because it was stitched together with hope, duct tape, and nightly builds that occasionally caught fire. But users kept saying it looked better, so I turned that chaos into something stable: V11.
Fair warning: it will generate different results than previous versions, thanks to its upgraded base.

This version brings better realism, refined skin textures, better lighting behaviour, and more consistent facial structure.

modelVersions.name: v10.0
modelVersions.description:
CyberRealistic Pony V10 is the next evolution. After extensive testing through the Catalyst beta branch, V10 brings lot of refinements, skin textures, better lighting behavior and more stable facial structure consistency. It’s more versatile, cleaner and retains the distinctive soft realism that defines the CyberRealistic Pony series.

As always, it remains free of charge, with no early access requirements or hidden conditions.

534506.name: Niji semi realism
534506.description:
Niji Semi-realism

V4.0 updated on 17/4/2026

This version is basically the upgraded edition of the LoRA — I fed it more images, switched the base model, upgraded the tagging system .

Result? It got smarter… and a bit picky 😏

I also split it into 3 personalities (styles), each with its own trigger word.

So yeah… you’re not dealing with one LoRA anymore, you’re dealing with a whole squad.

The Styles (choose your fighter)

1. SemiFrealism (F = Flat)

This one loves flat colors like anime and cartoons.

Clean, simple, and straight to the point — no extra drama.

Honestly, this is the favorite child of the LoRA.

2. SemiRrealism (R = Realistic)

Wants everything to look realistic and detailed.

Photorealistic? 3D? Fancy lighting? Say less.

This one tries a bit too hard to be real… but it works.

3. SemiNrealism (N = Normal)

The chill middle child.

Not too flat, not too realistic — just vibing in between.

This is the default mood if you don’t pick a side.

Important stuff (read this or suffer 😭)

If the result looks weird… it’s not the LoRA, it’s you 💀

Add proper keywords. The model is smart, but not a mind reader.

Don’t just spam one style and expect magic — experiment like a normal human.

Final warning ⚠️

This LoRA is powerful… but only if you know how to talk to it.

Treat it right, and it’ll cook 🔥

Use bad prompts… and it will absolutely embarrass you.

556479.name: MGE Monster Girls
556479.description:
Last updated - 21/03/2026

I now have a Ko-fi for those that'd like to support my projects. While nothing there will remain exclusive, you'll get more substantial progress updates each month, and access to my own non-AI images at higher tiers.

Model description:

This is an attempt at an all-in-one lora for generating the monster girls of MGE, which allows for extensive customisation and feature-mixing. Refer to these articles for more information. Not everything works perfectly, so good results may require a bit of experimentation at times.

For updates on future versions, I generally post what I'm working on in my profile's announcement tab. It'll probably be MGE-related, but in addition to this one I occasionally make standalone concept-loras for monsters that would benefit from having one.

Background info:

"MGE" Refers to the monster girl encyclopedia book series by Kenkou Cross. For more information, see the MGE wiki here.

Additionally, while I'm not affiliated with them, the wiki recently opened donations so you can donate here if you'd like. They have a $12/month upkeep cost apparently, so I'm sure they'd appreciate the generosity. Be sure to mention where you got the link from too, for the model's sake :).

"Non-entertainment" usage:

Any "commercial projects" must be transformative, so no posting your results to Patreon and asking for money. Using it as part of a larger project is fine though, and I'm happy to help if you reach out with questions, or simply to tell me an interesting use-case you have. Bear in mind that if you're creating stuff linked to MGE specifically, they must also adhere to the creator of MGE's terms here.

Actual artists:

If any artists dislike the idea of their stuff being used for this (and you've drawn things from MGE), let me know and I'll avoid your work. I haven't done this preemptively due to the huge number of dead source links in the archive, but if you reach out (with some proof of identity to avoid trolls) I'll remove any of your content from my dataset/s.

Reuploads:

If you reupload this, please link both this model page and my taglist for ease of use. I'd also appreciate you letting me know where you've uploaded it, so I can keep track of where it's being used.

modelVersions.name: V5.2 - Illustrious
modelVersions.description:
All of the changes from the Pony version of V5.2 apply, plus a few more minor changes. (I didn't want to spend an extra 4000-ish buzz on a retrain for the other version, but future versions should be identical.)

Differences from V5.2 Pony:

General Illustrious vs Pony observations:

  • Generally much more consistent, but in my experience requires more specific details to look good. I normally have to specify things like eye colour, facial expressions, arm positions and similar things with Illustrious to get a decent image, whereas Pony generally gives you decent ones with or without specification. With them though, the end result is almost always better with Illustrious.

  • Some styles work better with one, some with the other, although performance will vary with the specific model used regardless. Below is a list of all the styles that worked for each model. For Illustrious I used "NTR mix XIII" for testing, and for Pony I used "WAI-ANI-NSFW V12". (Mostly repeats, sorry. I can't change the colour of text in model updates, which is what I would've done to show version compatibility)

Illustrious:

  • MGE artstyle

  • CG artstyle

  • Merunyaa artstyle

  • Monorus artstyle

  • Barbariank artstyle

  • Sketch

  • Lineart

  • Pixel art

Pony:

  • MGE artstyle

  • CG artstyle

  • Merunyaa artstyle

  • Barbariank artstyle

  • Pixel art

Dataset changes (from V5.2 Pony):

  • Lilim horns aren't (as) broken this time, I removed too many images from the other version's dataset.

  • The "extremely long tongue" the karakasa-obake has been changed to "one tentacle" instead, and uses things like "loose tentacle hug" where appropriate. It still doesn't work to the extent I'd like, but the core features at-least appear now.

  • Separated a few species' ears using "floppy animal ears", where before things like "dog ears", "pig ears" and so on only used "animal ears".

  • Added "thick hair" to many of the slime girls, to give a bit more control over the hair design.

This version is mainly to fix the things V5.1 struggles with, and should (hopefully) give more consistent results. Also, I randomly decided to tag the raiju's legs as "calfhigh fur" instead, if you're after that specific leg style.

The style also works a lot better now, if you use "mge artstyle", and obviously higher weight increases the strength. From my limited testing, the style tends to give less detail the stronger it gets (see the newest manticore/dark slime images), but I'd assume using better prompts, different models or other loras should all be able to mitigate that.

Things I'm aware of:

- General inconsistencies - Fixed, see model description for tag order fixes

- Furless bunyips

- Winged arms not working

- "Full body" significantly reducing detail

- "MGE style" decreasing fidelity

- Slime girls always being opaque - (Sort of) fixed by tag order changes

- Gremlins having green skin

- Manticore tails being bad (this is likely to stay here for a while...)

- Characters having inconsistent features/clothing across multi-scene images (doujins, refrence sheets, etc)

- Smeared eyes/mouth (try "smeared eyes/mouth" in negatives, potential fix for no/bad pupils.)

- Raiju hair color inconsistency (which the AI kindly reminded me of by itself.) - Fixed, use tags: gradient hair, glowing hair, "x" hair to "y" hair (x and y being colors, scalp to tip)

- Bad barometz fruit (girl herself tends to be good)

- A few people have requested smaller file sizes, so I'll look into that too. Bear in mind, I'm a free user that can't train models locally (afaik, that is. If anyone knows of an AMD-compatble kohya alternative, lmk), so it'll likely be a while before I get anywhere with this.

Compared to V4.0, this version produces slightly cleaner outputs with a small step away from realism. The goal was never to “fix” or replace V4.0, but to offer an alternative flavor for users who prefer a more polished, semi-realistic look in certain scenarios.

V4.5 is best seen as a preference-based update: some scenes and styles benefit from the cleaner rendering, while others may still look better with the more realistic character of V4.0.

Bad Dream + Unrealistic Dream

(Negative Embeddings, make sure to grab BOTH)

Do you like what I do? Consider supporting me on Patreon 🅿️ or feel free to buy me a coffee ☕


Since I was refactoring my usual negative prompt with FastNegativeEmbedding, why not do the same with my super long DreamShaper negative prompts?

I decided to condense them into 2 words which are the two embeddings presented here.

BadDream is good for "dreamshaper style" stuff, while UnrealisticDream is more suited for realistic images, but it's not standalone. You should use it together with BadDream or with other negative words.

They can also both be combined with FastNegativeEmbedding.

Enjoy!

77976.name: CyberRealistic Negative - Pony v2.0
77976.description:
Join The Tinkerer on Whop to get early releases, private pages, and the Tinkerer Discord role - all in one place. 👉 Join on Whop

This Negative Embedding (TI) can assist you in achieving a more realistic portrayal when prompting.

  • Pony: for Realistic Pony (like CyberRealistic Pony, but it should work with every Realistic Pony model)

  • SDXL: for SDXL versions

  • Realistic 1.5: use standard version (V1.0)

  • Semi-Realistic/Anime 1.5: use Anime version


How to use

Download the file and place it in your embeddings folder. If you're using A1111 Webui, the folder path would be stable-diffusion-webui/embeddings (refer to the documentation to find the correct location for embeddings for other programs).

Once the file is in the designated folder, utilize the embedding by entering its name into the "negative prompt" box instead of the usual prompt. The default names are CyberRealistic_Negative, or CyberRealistic_Negative_Anime, depending on the version you downloaded.
